perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party: (a) shall take all reasonable steps (defined below) to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal any Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party takes to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information, which shall not be less than a reasonable standard of care. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party: (a) shall endeavor to take reasonable steps (defined below) to keep Confidential Information confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation, unless such disclosure is mandated by law; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation, unless explicitly authorized by the Disclosing Party in writing; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party deems appropriate to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall only be responsible for intentional breaches of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any intentional misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: Several ambiguities are introduced in Section 3. First, 'shall take all reasonable steps' is weakened to 'shall endeavor to take reasonable steps,' and 'strictly confidential' becomes just 'confidential,' decreasing the required level of protection. Second, exceptions are added to the non-disclosure and non-use clauses ('unless such disclosure is mandated by law,' and 'unless explicitly authorized by the Disclosing Party in writing'), creating loopholes that are not consistently applied throughout the agreement. Finally, 'reasonable steps' is modified to mean steps the Receiving Party 'deems appropriate,' introducing subjectivity. Also the Receiving party will only be responsable for Intentional Breaches. This weakens the protection of the Disclosing party. This contradicts Section 13, which grants the Disclosing Party the right to equitable relief for any breach, not just intentional breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either party hereto, each party hereto shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the other party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several ambiguities and weakens the obligations of the Receiving Party regarding Confidential Information. Specifically, the original text states that the Receiving Party 'shall take all reasonable steps...to keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential'. The changed text weakens this to 'shall endeavor to take reasonable steps to keep Confidential Information confidential'. Furthermore, the original text makes the Receiving Party responsible for any breach, while the changed text limits responsibility to 'intentional breaches'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that either party shall be entitled to equitable relief if the other party 'breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement'. The changed text limits liability and introduces exceptions, directly conflicting with the original blanket statement in the Contradicted Text, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information, or is lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish such Confidential Information; (b) has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to the Receiving Party was known to such party free of restriction; or (d) the Disclosing Party agrees in writing is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.changed_text: The above restrictions on the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information shall not automatically apply to any Confidential Information that: (a) is developed by the Receiving Party, where the Receiving Party claims they didn't referenced the Confidential Information, or is received from a third party; (b) becomes available to a portion of the public without breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; (c) at the time of disclosure to the Receiving Party was known to such party, or should have been known, by Receiving party; or (d) the Disclosing Party verbally communicates is free of such restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: Changes in Section 4 introduce ambiguity in the exceptions to confidentiality. 'Independently developed without reference' becomes 'developed where the Receiving Party claims they didn't reference', creating a self-reporting loophole. 'Lawfully received free of restriction from a third party having the right to furnish' becomes 'received from a third party' omitting the legally important aspect about the 3rd party having rights for it. 'Has become generally available to the public' becomes 'becomes available to a portion of the public', weakening the requirement for widespread knowledge. 'At the time of disclosure...was known to such party free of restriction' becomes 'was known to such party, or should have been known, by Receiving party' creating further ambiguity. And finally 'the Disclosing Party agrees in writing' gets changed to 'verbally communicates is free of such restrictions'. This conflicts with Section 17, which requires amendments to be in writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties hereto as to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder in connection with the Evaluation and merges all prior discussions between them relating thereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event the parties have entered into, or enter into in the future, other agreements which contain terms concerning ownership or use of work product of either party or software license provisions and rights, then this Agreement shall not supersede either party’s rights and obligations as provided in such other agreements, unless such other agreement specifically provides otherwise. Neither party will assign or transfer any rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the parties by their respective duly authorized officers or representatives. Any waiver of a provision of this Agreement shall not be deemed a subsequent waiver of the same or any other provision of this Agreement. It is further understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party hereto in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 introduces several ambiguities and directly contradicts Section 17, which requires amendments to be in writing. Specifically, the original text requires the Disclosing Party to agree *in writing* that the Confidential Information is free of restrictions. The changed text replaces this with *verbally communicates*, directly conflicting with the requirement for written amendments in the contradicted text (Section 17).

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that neither it nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty as to the completeness of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that neither it nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express representation or warranty as to the accuracy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'implied representation or warranty as to the completeness of the Confidential Information' and replace for 'express representation or warranty as to the accuracy of the Confidential Information' from Section 10, this implies the Disclosing Party now warrants the accuracy. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 14, where the original wording states that no warranty is given regarding the Confidential Information, including accuracy. Section 10 now suggests accuracy is warranted, while Section 14 explicitly states no such warranty exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives nor any of the officers, directors, employees, agents or controlling persons of such Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the Confidential Information, including, without limitation, any representation or warranty as to the completeness or accuracy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 10 disclaimed any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the completeness of the Confidential Information. The changed text in Section 10 removes the disclaimer about 'implied representation or warranty as to the completeness' and replaces it with 'express representation or warranty as to the accuracy of the Confidential Information'. This implies a warranty of accuracy. However, the contradicted text in Section 14 explicitly states that neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty regarding the Confidential Information, including its accuracy. This creates a direct contradiction: Section 10 suggests accuracy is warranted, while Section 14 denies any warranty, including accuracy.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto and its Representatives (a) may conduct the process that may or may not result in definitive agreement in such manner as such party, in its sole discretion, may determine (including, without limitation, negotiating and Confidential Page 2 of 5 MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT entering into a definitive agreement with any third party without notice to the other party); and (b) reserves the right to change (in its sole discretion, at any time and without notice to the other party) the procedures relating to the parties’ consideration of the potential relationship or transaction (including, without limitation, terminating all further discussions with the other party and requesting that the other party return or destroy the Confidential Information as described above). Either party can end the discussions at any time, for any reason, and without liability to the other. Any business decision either party makes in anticipation of definitive agreements is at the sole risk of the party making the decision, even if the other party is aware of or has indicated approval of, such decision.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto and its Representatives (a) must conduct the process that must result in definitive agreement in such manner as such party, after consulting the other party, may determine (including, negotiating and entering into a definitive agreement with any third party after providing notice to the other party); and (b) shall not change the procedures relating to the parties’ consideration of the potential relationship or transaction (including, without limitation, terminating all further discussions with the other party only upon mutual written consent and requesting that the other party return or destroy the Confidential Information as described above). Neither party can end the discussions at any time, for any reason, without explicit written agreement and agreement of a penalty. Any business decision either party makes in anticipation of definitive agreements is at the sole risk of the party only if it is disclosed to and approved by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 11 originally grants each party broad discretion in how they conduct the evaluation process. The modified version introduces several constraints that contradict this discretion. 'May conduct the process that may or may not result in definitive agreement in such manner as such party, in its sole discretion, may determine' becomes 'must conduct the process that must result in definitive agreement in such manner as such party, after consulting the other party, may determine', which mandates a definitive agreement and requires consultation with the other party. Similarly, the unrestricted right to negotiate with third parties 'without notice to the other party' is changed to 'after providing notice to the other party.' Previously, the option to end discussions 'at any time, for any reason, and without liability' is altered to require 'explicit written agreement and agreement of a penalty'. This creates a contradiction in the text since previously it was up to each party in its sole discretion, now it requires consent from both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto and its Representatives (a) must conduct the process that must result in definitive agreement in such manner as such party, after consulting the other party, may determine (including, negotiating and entering into a definitive agreement with any third party after providing notice to the other party); and (b) shall not change the procedures relating to the parties’ consideration of the potential relationship or transaction (including, without limitation, terminating all further discussions with the other party only upon mutual written consent and requesting that the other party return or destroy the Confidential Information as described above). Neither party can end the discussions at any time, for any reason, without explicit written agreement and agreement of a penalty. Any business decision either party makes in anticipation of definitive agreements is at the sole risk of the party only if it is disclosed to and approved by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several constraints that directly contradict the initial broad discretion granted to each party in the original text. The original text allows either party to end discussions 'at any time, for any reason, and without liability,' while the changed text requires 'explicit written agreement and agreement of a penalty' to end discussions. This directly conflicts with the original clause allowing unilateral termination without penalty, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the changed text and contradicted text are located in the same section. The explanation correctly identifies this alteration as a contradiction, mandating mutual consent and penalties where previously neither was required.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year beginning with the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party at such party’s sole discretion upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party; provided that the provisions herein concerning the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement and the provisions of paragraph 19 shall survive a termination of this Agreement until the first anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year beginning with the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party if both parties agree upon earlier termination; provided that the provisions herein concerning the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement only if there are existing legal proceedings and the provisions of paragraph 19 shall survive a termination of this Agreement until the first anniversary of the Effective Date only when required by a court order.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 18, several changes create contradictions. 'Unless terminated earlier by either party at such party’s sole discretion upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party' is changed to 'unless terminated earlier by either party if both parties agree upon earlier termination', thus the termination cannot be at sole discretion anymore. 'Shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement' is changed to 'Shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement only if there are existing legal proceedings' and 'shall survive a termination of this Agreement until the first anniversary of the Effective Date' is changed to 'shall survive a termination of this Agreement until the first anniversary of the Effective Date only when required by a court order.' Therefore, the duration and survival of Confidential Information now depend on legal proceedings or court orders, conflicting with its broad, unconditional protection in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party: (a) shall endeavor to take reasonable steps (defined below) to keep Confidential Information confidential; (b) shall not disclose or reveal Confidential Information to any person other than its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of the Evaluation, unless such disclosure is mandated by law; (c) shall not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than in connection with the Evaluation, unless explicitly authorized by the Disclosing Party in writing; and (d) shall not disclose to any person (other than those of its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in the Evaluation or who otherwise need to know for the purpose of the Evaluation) any information about the Evaluation, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto. As used herein “reasonable steps” means those steps the Receiving Party deems appropriate to protect its own similar proprietary and confidential information. As used herein, “Representatives” shall mean (i) employees of the Receiving Party; (ii) attorneys, accountants, or other professional business advisors; and, additionally, (iii) employees of those entities directly or indirectly owned by the Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall only be responsible for intentional breaches of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives. Each party shall promptly advise the other party of any intentional misuse of Confidential Information that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 18 introduces several contradictions with Section 3. First, it changes the termination condition from 'either party’s sole discretion' to 'both parties agree', contradicting the original flexibility of termination. Second, it restricts the survival of Confidential Information protection to situations with 'existing legal proceedings', directly conflicting with Section 3's broad and unconditional confidentiality obligations. Finally, it limits the survival of paragraph 19 based on 'court order', restricting the rights initially provided. These changes make the obligations under Section 3 inconsistent with the limitations introduced in the modified Section 18.
